text: Halcyon

Halcyon is the capital of the empire of Apollyean. A wall-less city of 80,000, Halcyon sits  beside the Crystal Falls, on the shore of the Lake of the Ancestors, and at the start of the  Mirrorbrook. A common saying of Halcyon is to see your forebears in your own reflection.  This may be in part due to the locality of the Reflections’ Crest, the cliff face that is the end  of the El’drath’el Range. In this cliff, it's said one may see  spirits floating through the  throughways and streets. One day's walk from the ocean. Halcyon is there because it's the  last navigable point on the Mirrorbrook.

Halcyon is very much a melting pot of the emperor. Many see it as a bastion of hope, a  place to find themselves anew in its crystalline cliffs and waters. Lorded over by the  stalwart, stubborn, heirless King Appoleas Apollyean III, a human of 53 years, and by his  side Evytte, his regal, fancy queen. The king and queen take a hands-on approach to  Halcyon, often hearing the pleas of citizens and immigrants alike in their court. When they  are unable, the Crystal Council will hear them instead. When there is trouble, Aylin’s  Stalwarts will take care to clean it up. Named after their founding member who lost his life  defending the cliffside from trolls.

A hub of trade and commerce, little is actually produced from within the boundaries of  Halcyon. Food is mainly delivered through the trade routes along the cliffs, which Halcyon  can afford with its moderately vast wealth and power. Life is largely good within Halcyon,  though trouble may brew where the reflections cannot touch.

Daytime in Halcyon varies largely from district to district. City-wide, however, shop  windows are glass, and they are not guarded during the night. Theft is an offense  punishable by death within Halcyon, as to siege what does not belong to you is a direct  refusal of what has been given by your ancestors.

Many in the Metamorphic peddle their wares on the street, work at the Docks, or travel  across the Mirrorbrook to the Witio-Blessed Mines. At night, the Metamorphic District is  silent, save for the footfalls of those brave few dark-seers who conduct business in shadow.

The Sedimentary appears neigh-uninhabited by night, as the lightless streets grant little  protection to those who wander amongst them. Those who do travel at night often do so  via the guidance of Illiard and Helwy, as it is customary that disciples of the Divine Marriage  pray each night at high midnight.

The Igneous District is the only district wherein torches are permissible to be lit in the  dark. Even so, dramatically few lights grace even these cobblestone streets. It is said that  spirits and ghosts traverse those through ways when light doesn’t shine.
Events
Weekly:

●​ On the 7th day of the week, a coliseum event occurs  ●​ On the 1st day of the week, the tithe collector visits the Metamorphic District  ●​ On the 2nd day of the week, the tithe collector visits the Sedimentary District  ●​ On the 3rd day of the week, the tithe collector visits the Igneous Disctrict  ●​ On the 5th day, the sphinx comes to deal justice in the Sedimentary district  ●​ Every other 5th day, the Metamorphic district hears civilian cases
Daily:
●​ At sundown, all lights are extinguished.
Quest
Nine Shards of Halcyon

1.​ In the church of the dead  2.​ In the protection of the largest merchant in Halcyon  3.​ Held by the reigning victor of the Colosseum   4.​ Kept safe behind the crystal falls  5.​ Held by the mage who rules over the Sedimentary District  6.​ Kept safe in the artist who sees the realms  7.​ Deified by the underdwellers  8.​ In the king’s chambers  9.​ In the great hall of the School of Abjuration
The Three Districts of Halcyon
The Igneous District
This is where the nobles live and where the caste is. This is largely ruled by the King’s  Counsel, and oftentimes by the King himself.
Within the Igneous District there are three boroughs:
The Magma Gate

Heralded as the oldest district in Halcyon, the Magma gate is constructed from crimson  stone and crystal that glows faintly at dusk. This borough is home to some of the oldest  families in Halcyon and numerous torchlit parades.
Ashwall Promenade

Known for clean, cobbled roads lined with polished obsidian statues, the Ashwall is for the  most exclusive shopping in the entirety of Apollyean. Magic merchants often find their way  here, under the scrutinous eye of those who came before.
Pyroclast Yard
The Sedimentary District

This is where those who have lived in Halcyon for generations or plan to live in Halcyon for  the rest of their lives choose to settle. The buildings here tend to be tall, oftentimes  multiple stories, though not excessively broad. Many choose to have their common areas  on the first floor and their sleeping chambers on the upper stories.

The Sedentary District is looked after by the Great Mage, a largely benevolent ruler who it  is said to have knowledge of the Collapse. It is also said that court is held once weekly in the  presence of Indracus, the great Sphinx of El’drath’el.
Within the Sedentary District there are three boroughs:
Layerfall Commons
Aside from the castle, the Layerfall Commons are the highest spot in Halcyon, offering  unparalleled views of the city and the Lake of the Ancestors.
Fossil Walk
Crowded with markets, shops, and shrines built into the bones of ancient beasts  discovered, or slaughtered, during the city’s expansion.
Stratahearth
Known for terraced roadhouses and intricate mosaics recounting the history of Halcyon,  the Walk is known as a gathering place for storytellers and historians.
The Metamorphic District

The life within the Metamorphic district is where the lower class folk tend to gather.  Vagrants, immigrants, and tradesmen find themselves within the winding streets leading  towards the docs. Here, many find their lives and work that will either lead them into the  upper crust of Halcyon, or their way out of the Appollyean Kingdom.

The Metamorphic District is looked after by the Ancestors’ Counsel. This council is  constituted by the Lord of Commerce, the Lord of Transit, the Lord of Justice, and the  Ancestor, with the latter role being filled by a spirit of one of the Lords that has passed  away.
Within the Metamorphic District there are three boroughs:
Marblemorph

The Marblemorph is bustling with stonecarvers, blacksmiths, masons, and wandering  laborers. The alleys of this borough are also lined with every-shifting graffiti, making it  difficult for those unfamiliar to find their way.
Garnet Hollow
The Hollow is known to be the city’s hub of trade, commerce, and the location of the  airship dock.
Quartzshift
Quartzshift is known for its deep alleys, pickpockets, and whispers that echo down the  throughways of schemes, plots, and wishes. Many say the Shift leads to the underworld,  though none say where.
Holy Places of Halcyon
Halcyon is home to several religious temples, and a shrine to any god or goddess may be  found throughout the city. The most prominent temples are as follows:
The Temple to Witio - The Font of Molten Wisdom

Exterior: Black basalt columns crown the temple, veins of glowing, living crystal winding  upward like molten rivers; at dusk, the entrance shimmers and pulses, promising secrets  only revealed by wisdom and fire.

Interior: Flickering braziers cast golden light onto groves of hanging scrolls and books; a  low, eternal hum accompanies every step, scholars consulting illuminated manuscripts or  whispering riddles to the god of knowledge.
Important People:

●​ High Sage Calaxes Vedran: Robed in white and red, his eyes alight with learning, he  interprets the cryptic wisdoms of Witio and oversees all initiates.  ●​ Dalae Draventh: Keeper of keys and riddles, angular-faced and soft-spoken, she  guides guests through magical trials to prove their worth.  ●​ Archivist Loryn Feldspar: Elderly halfling, devoted to preserving every piece of  knowledge; she ensures the temple’s archives are safe from vandalism or theft.  ●​ Asherun Rhyll: Arcane prodigy who debates philosophy in the atrium, known for  weaving spells into daily parables.  ●​ Garanth Solanar: Temple guardian, imposing but gentle, prefers meditation to  violence—his ceremonial staff burnished with obsidian.
The Temple to Baerwu - Spire of the Unbound Wind
Exterior: Towering, sky-blue spire, banners billowing in the perpetual breeze; the steps  upwards are open and airy, granting panoramic views across Halcyon and the Ancestors'  Lake below.

Interior: Wind chimes and cascading silks flutter overhead, sunlight pouring through  stained-glass stories on every wall; worshippers sing prayers to the wind, their voices  harmonizing with the breeze that never stills.
Important People:

●​ High Priestess Ythari Siltwind: With hair that ripples like rain, she leads morning  ceremonies and reads omens from the movement of wind and silk.  ●​ Stormchanter Vellis Mossbrook: Youthful bard whose ballads evoke the strength and  softness of wind; inspires citizens in festivals and troubles.  ●​ Keeper Tumla Ambervale: Persistent, elderly woman who tends the flying garden  and ensures the temple is open to all.

●​ Skywatcher Greylin Mottle: Halfling seer who charts wind patterns and sometimes  predicts fortune from dreams.  ●​ Novitiate Eliard Flint: Silent apprentice, tasked with cleaning and maintaining the  high spire; rumored to commune with wild birds.
The Temple to Eawin - Veilkeepers Sepulcher

Exterior: Carved from moonstone and jet, the sepulcher’s facade is adorned with motifs of  veils and flowing water, its entrance surrounded by murmuring, weeping willows; on dusk,  soft candles gleam on every step.

Interior: Incense cloaks shadowed alcoves—each lined with memorials and silken veils;  slow, haunting chants echo as worshippers honor the dead and pray to Eawin for memory,  forgiveness, and safe passage.
Important People:

●​ High Priestess Marina Idurin: Stern and empathetic, she guides the district’s rites for  mourning and sacrifice; her gaze reads the soul of each confessor.  ●​ Ralphie Idurin: The inn’s gentle dog, bringing comfort and warmth to grieving  mourners or lost children.  ●​ Mistress Sera Umberglass (Ancestor Spirit): Her pale presence floats during  midnight prayer, sometimes offering wisdom to those in deepest need.  ●​ Soulkeeper Gersa Cottect: Tiefling whose voice is soft and slow, she records the  stories of the departed and keeps the sepulcher’s torch lit.  ●​ Acolyte Pemble Truce: Young attendant who weaves and hangs new veils, always  careful never to disturb the city’s ancestral peace.
The Temple to Altent - The Hall of Fallen Blades
Located in The Below.
Houses of Halcyon
House Apollyean: The ruling dynasty of Halcyon, with an unbroken bloodline. King  Appoleas III and Queen Evytte oversee Halcyon’s unity, justice, and military.

House of Umberglass, the House of the Ancestors: House Umberglass is where those who  deemed they still have work to do on the mortal realm have settled. These spirits oversee  justice throughout the city, and nominate a head of the Ancestors Council.
The Noble Houses
House Solanar: Ancient knights and warriors who are eternal protectors of the king and  crown. The most powerful house in Halcyon.
House Vedran: The architects of the Igneous and Sedimentary districts, responsible for  collecting tithes and managing the landscape of Halcyon.
House Draventh: The masters of trade and commerce within Halcyon. Goods rarely come  in or out of Halcyon without their knowing. Their motto is “The Flame Endures.”
The Old-Blood Houses

House Feldspar: An ancient farming house, with their ancestors thought to build the first  terraces of Halcyon. This fiercely traditional, matriarchal house presents the high  priestesses of the Harvest.
House Rhyll: The scholars of Halcyon, they are also the record-keepers and official  historians of the city. Their archives contain lost magic and sealed family secrets.
House Siltwind: The community leaders of the city, the Siltwinds hold feasts and spiritual  celebrations, and are generally beloved of the city. Known for their devotion to Baerwu.
The Merchant Guilds
House Gemwright: Gemcutters and artificers, they are known to be jealous of the Igneous  houses, tight-lipped, and secretive. Their sigil is the sapphire owl.
House Forgevein: The head of the masons and builders guild within Halcyon. They are  responsible for building houses and maintaining the infrastructure of Halcyon.
House Mottle: Powerful traders and rumored smugglers, Mottle is known to have fingers in  every market across the city and operate a network of informants. (Secretly also houses the  thieves’ guild)
Information to be added:
The Death Bed Inn:
Outside: Lanterns dissolve into morning mist above its ancient timber door, bones  engraved on the windowsill catch early sun.
Inside: Shelves crowded with curios and foodstuffs blend seamlessly into prayer shrines  and backroom soup pots.

Run by Mateus and Marina Idurin, along with their dog Ralphie. The Death Bed Inn is  connected to the temple to Eawin, as Marina is the head priestess of that temple. A  punishment of those who are to be sent to the gallows is to be sent into Marina’s flock,  where they will be ritually sacrificed. Marina is also known for hosting crochet nights and  community kitchen events for those who have come upon hard times in Halcyon. Mateus  runs the Inn and the general store, where most non-magical items are sold at a reasonable  price. Mateus is a follower of Munder, and as such he also operates the Inn on a barter  system, knowledge for rooms or items. Mateus is bereft to magic.
The Below:

Underneath the city there is a vast maze of interconnected tunnels, cellars, sewers, and  catacombs which form a sparsely connected dungeon underneath Halcyon. There is a  temple to Altent down there, though that is known to nearly all of the populace.
The Arcanum of Astral Nexus:

The Reflections Crest was created by an ancient fallen city of the sky, that of  Audopopadopolis. Underneath that once great city, lies the now forgotten college of  Abjuration. The Arcanum of Astral Nexus: No one knows what befall the fortress of the  Abjurers. What is known is that within those walls, students would gather to fabricate  impervious defenses, both magical and physical.
The Coliseum: The Arena of Shattered Echoes

On the border of the Sedimentary and Metamorphic districts sits a megalithic coliseum  wherein fights to the death between gladiators, animals, and magical beasts alike take part.  Within this coliseum, there is a weekly event, rotating each week between a fight to the  death, an epic play, and military exercises.

Exterior: Cyclopean stone arches loom over the border of the Metamorphic and  Sedimentary Districts, banners snap in wind above thick iron gates; the roar inside seems  to vibrate even the stillness outside, and the coliseum's shadow covers dozens of merchant  carts and festival tents.
Interior: Tiered stands crowd with shouting crowds; the fighting pit glows with enchanted  sand, and an ancient mural recounts hundreds of years of gladiatorial triumphs—and  tragedies.
Important People:

●​ Palvo Jestil: Scarred, flamboyant dwarf who runs sign-ups and event rosters—his  stories and bets are near legendary.  ●​ Reigning Victor Kessyn Marblemorph: Surly, bronze-skinned champion of the  current season, always hunting new challengers or rivals.  ●​ Mistress Nalan Siltwind: Overseer of dramatic plays and ceremonies, directing  actors and gladiators alike.  ●​ Quartermaster Cardin Voss: One-eyed weapons master, ensures all arms and gear  are ready for the combatants—he has the quiet authority of a storm about to break.  ●​ Beastmaster Greydus Pipe: Tall, stooped half-orc charged with caring for the  coliseum’s stable of magical beasts and arena animals.
The Docks:

Halcyon’s medieval docks are a living tapestry stretched across the stony shore of the Lake  of the Ancestors. Timber piers, some centuries old, jut into deep, flickering waters where  fishing boats, merchant barges, and broad-hulled ferries sway at rest. The air smells of  brine, pitch, and cured fish, mingled with the rich aromas of tallow lanterns burning at  sundown. Stone steps descend from the market square, lined with grit and tangled ropes,  where sailors and dockhands haggle and sing among crates marked with foreign runes and  the city’s obsidian seal. At dawn, fishermen mend their nets with practiced patience,  children chase gulls for luck, and traders unload silks, salt, and iron by hand, piling goods in  shadowy warehouses whose doors never sleep. Lanterns twinkle from masthead to  mooring post, illuminating knots of travelers, scribes tallying manifests, and the slouching  backs of local watchmen who eye the night’s business with silent vigilance.
Key People of Halcyon’s Medieval Docks

●​ Temra Garnet: The dockmaster. Broad-hipped, slate-gray hair tightly braided, cloak  heavy from years of rain and spray. Her commands carry over the hubbub as she  sorts berths and settles disputes with a voice that brooks no argument.  ●​ Barlo Fen: Chief labor foreman. Barrel-chested, bald with eyes like polished agate.  Wears a patched jerkin and fingerless gloves, quick with jokes but quicker to bark  orders at loafing hands or smugglers testing his patience.

●​ Orik Slatehand: Head stevedore. Towering goliath, arms crisscrossed with faded  runic tattoos and scars from decades hauling loads. Believes in fair pay and hard  work, settling quarrels with his presence alone or an iron-stiff handshake.  ●​ Simi Ambervale: Street peddler and errand girl. Petite halfling with fiery hair tied in  ribbons, bouncing from sailor to merchant with baskets of fresh bread, salted meats,  and whispered news, always ready to help or hide depending on the hour.  ●​ Willa Peach: Herbalist and dockside healer. Magenta-braided gnome with an apron  covered in green stains, known for her healing teas and poultices. Mends minor  wounds from splintered decks and scuffles, and trades friendly counsel for gossip  during the slow hours.  ●​ Marn Crow: Scribe and tallyman. Gaunt human in ink-stained doublet, spectacles  hanging by a thread. Keeps obsessive records of each ship’s manifest and is trusted  by captains, for his memory is sharper than any blade in the city.
Garnet Hollow Transfers (Airship & Goods Broker)
Outside: Brass gears and ropes coil around a bustling dock platform, fumes and commotion  thick as morning fog.
Inside: Ledgers and manifests spill across every surface, littered with tokens from far-off  lands and ticking clocks.
Shopkeeper: Temra Garnet—broad-hipped, gray-braided, her gaze measuring more than  goods in every glance.
Veins and Chains:
A tattoo parlor and jewelry shop known by a mysterious woman from across the ocean.
Outside: Emerald glass and painted symbols glimmer across the dusk-soaked brick,  mysterious voices heard within.
Inside: Flickering candles and sweet incense reveal a riot of vibrant inks, and shadowy  jewelry glitters on velvet.
Marblemorph Curiosity
Outside: Pillars of swirling, painted marble frame a crooked doorway, colors ever-shifting in  sunlight.
Inside: Trinket-filled shelves, odd artifacts on velvet, eccentric displays sculpted from stone  and bone.
Shopkeeper: Vax Marblemorph—paint-spattered, wild-haired, eyes darting for inspiration,  always ready for the next new story.
Siltwind Scrolls & Sundries
Outside: Faded awning stamped with ancient glyphs, a bell chimes in fog as neighbors  bustle past.
Inside: Walls lined with parchment and bundles of quills, air tinged with cedar and  whispered rumors.
Shopkeeper: Theska Siltwind—short, round-faced, with ink-stained fingers and a voice that  carries wisdom through laughter.
Mossbrook Market
Outside: Garlands of wildflowers dangle over a clutter of open carts, laughter and scents  swirl in the lively street.
Inside: Checkerboard tiles, baskets overflowing with bread, roots, and herbs beneath a  stained glass skylight.
Shopkeeper: Fio Mossbrook—rosy-cheeked cook, apron dusted with flour, and eyes bright  as fresh-baked morning.
Obsidian Armaments
Outside: Smoky iron sign swings on blackened chains, blocky façade carved with runes,  guarded by stern stone lions.
Inside: Shelves glimmer with sectioned blades, polished bucklers, and rare obsidian  arrowheads, all bathed in cool torchlight.
Shopkeeper: Cardin Voss—tall, one-eyed, steel-gray hair in braids, his voice gravelly yet  inviting.
Strata’s Thread
Outside: Laundry lines crisscross the alley beside a painted door, colored fabrics ripple like  banners in the breeze.
Inside: Spools of linen and velvet pile high, each corner buzzing with whispered gossip and  the flick of sewing needles.
Shopkeeper: Liala Strata—lean, sharp-eyed, her hands quick with needle or secrets passed  in silk folds.
NPCs
King Appoleas Apollyean III: The stalwart, stubborn, and heirless human king of Halcyon.  Rules with pride in the city’s traditions and regularly hears the pleas of citizens.
Queen Evytte Apollyean: The regal and flamboyant queen, renowned for her involvement  in city affairs and elegant style.

Mateus Idurin: Broad-shouldered, slate-eyed, with stubble as rough as old cobbles and a  perpetual smudge of flour. Weathered hands cradle stories and secrets. Stout as an old oak,  with salt-flecked hair and a gaze like deep river stone.

Marina Idurin: Storm-gray hair in a tight bun, white skin framed by priestly robes; warm,  crinkled eyes above a mouth set for grieving and encouragement. Silver-haired and cloaked  in faded velvet, her eyes warm as dusk lanterns.

Ralphie Idurin: Curly haired family dog of the Idurins, beloved by all. With hair as black as  night and a tail that sweeps like a broom through the travelers’ dust, he is often found  comforting guests or livening up the inn’s evenings. Shaggy-furred, black as night, exuberant  tail and soulful eyes that see kindness in all.

Lord Harrow Fenn: Grizzled dwarf merchant, sharp-tongued, runs Garnet Hollow’s trade  hub; keeps an eye on market trends and under-the-table deals. Stocky, cinnamon-bearded,  amber-skinned, eyes sharp as fresh-cut garnets beneath a battered merchant’s cap. Broad  and bristling, with a cinnamon beard and merchant’s rings glittering in every gesture.

Mistress Ythari Siltwind: Tall half-elf woman, master of street routes and transit permits,  renowned for her patience and encyclopedic memory of Halcyon's alleys and shortcuts.  Tall, willowy, with river-stone hair and moon-bright irises—her robes trail like whispered
secrets in dusk alleys. Graceful, tall as twilight, copper-skinned with river-water hair and  robes sewn from night breezes.

Justice Caleos Obsidian: Retired city guard, stoic and slow to anger, trusted to arbitrate  lesser disputes and take confessions after dark. Iron-jawed, silver-stubbled, clad in black  magistrate’s leathers; hands like granite and a gaze weighing every soul’s truth. Chiseled  features shadowed in gray, voice deep as the city’s old stone, eyes unwavering as a judge’s  verdict.

The Ancestor—Specter of Sera Umberglass: Ghostly mentor to the council, visible only at  midnight prayer; revered for insights from the spirit realm and connection to the district’s  ancestral past. Diaphanous, luminous—her presence an echo of violet and pearl, drifting  with a hush through candlelit mist. Pale violet mist shaped like a serene matron, her eyes soft  moons, her form shifting in every candle’s glow.

Vax Marblemorph: Street artist known for ever-changing graffiti; eccentric, unpredictable,  offers coded clues for those looking to contact the city's underworld. Spry, paint-splashed;  angular features wreathed in wild green curls, fingers stained in every pigment at sunrise.  Spry frame, wild green hair, patchwork jacket speckled with paint and hinting of strange  murals.

Luran Forgevein: Energetic young mason, always recruiting day-laborers for projects.  Quick to gossip and always has a loose stone to fix or story to tell. Sunburnt youth, freckles  on a nose broken twice, chunky work boots, and arms ringed with dust-streaked muscle.  Bronzed and broad-shouldered, dust-streaked, hammer-scarred hands always mid-gesture,  quick grin under a freckled nose.

Drin Mottle: Wiry halfling trader with ties to much of the district's commerce—runs the  busiest peddler cart, rumored to secretly broker deals for House Mottle. Lean, quick-eyed  halfling, cheeks sharp and voice lilting, best coat stitched from ten markets’ worth of stolen  fabric. Lean, wiry, with laughter lines and a hat festooned with haggled trinkets; eyes sparkle  with mischief.

Anava "Veins" Chell: Foreign tattooist and jeweler who owns Veins and Chains. Her jewelry  is said to carry protective blessings, and her tattoos are sought by those wishing to  reinvent themselves upon arriving in Halcyon. White-skinned, with jet-black hair cropped  close; arms inked in twisting runes, jewelry dangling like starlight on midnight velvet.  Low-cut dress, bronze eyes. White-skinned, elegant, tattoos curling up sinewy arms; jewelry  catching every mote of candlelight.

Temra Garnet: Airship dock supervisor, gruff middle-aged woman who ensures goods  move smoothly through Garnet Hollow; will trade stories for favors and keeps a ledger of  debts. Sturdy, broad-hipped, slate-gray hair braided and dotted with ship charms; her  boots always splashed in oil and morning dew. Sturdy and stoic, short-cropped silver hair,  deep-set brown eyes, deck boots still stained with dock oil.

Mira Quartzshift: Secretive pickpocket and information broker; offers dubious "tours" of  the district’s shadowy backstreets for coin or secrets. Petite, hawk-nosed, eyes darting with  glassy energy, palms nimble and stained with ink and coin dust. Petite but fox-eyed, nimble  fingers clad in ink, her cloak always flowing and footsteps nearly silent.

Old Jerrik the Night-Seer: Blind prophet, guides travelers and late-night workers through  the district at sundown; known for poetic riddles and warnings about restless spirits.  Wrinkled as river driftwood, hair pale and unkempt, milky unseeing eyes over lips  whispering secrets only stars remember. Ancient and hunched, milky eyes haunting his  smile, skin crinkled like old parchment under starlit haze.

Fio Mossbrook: Street-corner cook, feeds hungry children and vagrants by day, moonlights  as a supplier of rare herbs and local gossip. Round-cheeked, always smiling, flour-dusted  apron, eyes golden with hearthlight and curiosity. Round-faced, rosy-cheeked, apron dusted  with flour, gleaming teeth and eyes full of sunrise optimism.

Liala Strata: Laundry mistress, her business acts as a front for relaying message drops  between districts and houses. Tall, wiry, fingers deft with folded linen; eyes like smoky  quartz, scanning for secrets as she works among billowing sheets. Tall and lean, eyes sharp  as flint, calloused hands dancing amid laundry lines and hushed secrets.
